# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '2' # YAML version declaration (optional, but recommended)
services:   // Defines the services to be run by docker-compose command in a file called `docker-compose.yml` or similar name at top level of your Dockerfile/project  root directory if not specified otherwise according their needs within this configuration and then will auto build them using default image settings
   redis:     // Name for our Redis service, it can be anything but we are choosing 'redis' here as per requirement. If you choose a different name make sure to update all references accordingly in your system  (optional) by changing `service_name` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
     image: redis    // Name/tag for Docker Redis Image, this is what will be used while running docker run. Here 'redis' tag as per requirement but any other available name would suffice according to your requirements (optional) by changing `image_name` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
     ports: // This part specifies how services should expose their network traffic outside Docker Compose, here we are exposing port 6379 inside our docker-compose.yml for Redis (optional) by changing `service_port` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
       - "6379:${PORT}"    // Exposes container ports to host machine, in this case it is exposing port 6379 as per requirement. If you want your app clients or applications can connect at different/specified 'port' here (optional) by changing `service_redisPort` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
     volumes:   // The data persistence for Redis, it is not created until specified in this section. If you want to persist redis database even if container stops or restarts then use volumne (optional) by changing `service_redisVolumes` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
       - ./data:/data/redis // This line is for linking data folder within docker-compose.yml to /path inside Reddis container, you can change this according your requirement (optional) by changing `service_linkedRedisData` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
     restart: "always"    // Determines how services are retried when they fail in production environment (required). If not specified redis service will keep running unless it is explicitly stopped. Here always tag for 'Always' mode as per requirement but you can use other values according to your need, like `unless-stopped` which would run the container if already exists and stop otherwise only when there was a failure (optional) by changing ‘restart_redisRestarts’ from below command
      - "6379"    // Container port in production mode as per requirement. If you want it to be specific for example 'my-port' then use this tag here accordingly if any and on top of the main configuration file (optional) by changing `service_redisPort` from below command
     networks:   /// This section is not mandatory but required when two or more services need access in network, you can define an interconnected service within redis-server part. Here for 'networking' as per requirement to be added if needed (optional) by changing `service_networks` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
       - app-net    // Name/tag or any name, according your requirements you can define it here too when two services are supposed interconnected with each other within redis server. For now we just use 'app' for our example (optional) by changing `service_redisNetworks` from below command and on top of the main configuration file
